channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro says the US is fabricating a war as aircraft carrier approaches
item.description: Venezuela's President Nicolás Maduro claims the U.S. is waging a war against him as the world's largest warship nears the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A bomb in Gaza's rubble wounds twins who thought it was a toy
item.description: Six-year-old twins in Gaza City were playing outside when they saw a round object and thought it was a toy.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump rips Canada's 'crooked' anti-tariffs ad as possibly AI
item.description: President Trump took another swing at Canada late Friday, calling a controversial anti-tariff ad featuring excerpts from a former President Reagan speech, "crooked" and "dishonest." He also suggested that the video was "possibly" generated by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: Voters in Ivory Coast have cast their ballots to elect a new leader as President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term.
item.dc:creator: Ope Adetayo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When President Donald Trump took the stage in Egypt to hail the U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas, a row of world leaders stood behind him like extras in a political drama. It was a telling image.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A vividly hued Picasso portrait of longtime muse and partner Dora Maar that had remained hidden from public view for more than eight decades sold Friday at auction for 32 million euros (about $37 million), including fees — surpassing expectations but far from the artist’s most expensive
item.dc:creator: OLEG CETINIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 48 bags of suspected human remains found at Mexican gravesite
item.description: Previous discoveries of bags found buried in Jalisco state gravesites have included bone fragments and human tissue.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Canada 'trying to illegally influence' Supreme Court on tariffs
item.description: President Trump defended his decision to call off trade negotiations with Canada on Friday, accusing the U.S.'s northern neighbor of attempting to "illegally influence" a pending Supreme Court case regarding tariffs. "CANADA CHEATED AND GOT CAUGHT!!!" Trump posted to Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: NATO fighter jets scrambled after Russian planes allegedly violate airspace
item.description: Spanish NATO jets were scrambled after two Russian aircraft reportedly violated Lithuanian airspace, escalating tensions amid the ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International Criminal Court judges reject jurisdiction challenge in Duterte case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — International Criminal Court judges on Thursday rejected a challenge to jurisdiction in the case of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, who is accused of involvement in dozens of killings as part of his so-called war on drugs when in office.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top UN court says Israel must allow UN relief agency to supply aid to Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Court of Justice said on Wednesday that Israel must allow the U.N. aid agency in Gaza, known as UNRWA, to provide humanitarian assistance to the Palestinian territory. The Hague-based court was asked late last year by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vance meets with Netanyahu, urges patience as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: After meeting with Israel's prime minister, Vance said the United States' role was to support and safeguard the ceasefire, not to "monitor a toddler.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: US military opens coordination center in Israel for Gaza stabilization and aid efforts amid ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. military created a Civil-Military Coordination Center near Gaza to oversee ceasefire implementation and facilitate humanitarian assistance from international partners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louvre director summoned by lawmakers after jewel theft
item.description: The Louvre is facing questions about lax security measures after thieves made off with priceless jewels.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kartalija, NewsNation

item.title: Vance warns Hamas as Gaza peace plan's Civilian Military Cooperation Center opens
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said that he's confident in "durable" peace amid the ceasefire, warning Hamas will be "obliterated" if it doesn't cooperate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican lime growers' leader found dead after anti-cartel statements
item.description: In interviews, Bernardo Bravo ripped into organized crime's "permanent commercial hijacking of any commercial activity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia Giuffre, Epstein and Prince Andrew accuser, gets 'final word' in new memoir
item.description: The memoir serves as a discussion of surviving abuse, fighting for justice and suffering at the hands of Prince Andrew and disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: US envoys visit Israel to bolster truce, and Gaza militants hand over a hostage's remains
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Two of U.S. President Donald Trump’s envoys traveled to Israel on Monday to shore up the ceasefire in Gaza, a day after deadly violence gave the fragile deal its first major test.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre heist thieves had 'inside help': Former jewel thief
item.description: Lawton described the operation as "very well planned" and explained that before pulling off a heist like this, "you have to know so much.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia recalls US ambassador after Trump's tariff threat, drug remarks
item.description: Trump also called Colombian's leftist President Gustavo Petro an "illegal drug leader" on Sunday, which Petro's government described as offensive.
item.dc:creator: Luis Jaime Acosta and Julia Symmes Cobb / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A cargo aircraft skids off a Hong Kong runway into the sea, killing 2 people
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cargo aircraft skidded off a Hong Kong runway into the sea when landing early Monday, killing two people. The Emirates flight, arriving from Dubai, United Arab Emirates, was landing at Hong Kong International Airport around 3:50 a.m., according to Hong Kong’s airport authority.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end the Russian invasion
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that the Donbas region of Ukraine should be “cut up," leaving most of it in Russian hands, to end a war that has dragged on for nearly four years. “Let it be cut the way it is,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
